subject: Calculus Prerequisite
question: For what values of $c$ is the improper integral $\int_0^\infty e^{cx}\,dx$ convergent?
explanation: We have $\int^\infty e^{cx}\,dx = \lim_{b\to\infty} \int^b e^{cx}\,dx = \lim_{b\to\infty} \frac{1}{c}e^{cx}\Big|^b = \lim_{b\to\infty} \frac{1}{c}(e^{cb} - 1) = -\frac{1}{c}$ provided that $c < 0$. Otherwise, i.e. if $c \ge 0$, then the improper integral is divergent. Remark 28.1 The previous two results are very useful and you may want to memorize them.
